Title: An Extracellular Siderophore Is Required to Maintain the Mutualistic Interaction of Epichloë festucae with Lolium perenne

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: P-glycoprotein-like multidrug resistance (MDR) subfamily ABC transporter
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: High-affinity iron acquisition in the symbiosis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. abcI is located upstream of sidN and appears to form a partial siderophore biosynthetic gene cluster. 2. It encodes a putative ABC transporter analogous in position/orientation to H. capsulatum ABC1. 3. No direct functional validation for epichloënin A or iron phenotype was reported in this paper.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as a clustered neighboring gene, not functionally tested as a primary experimental target.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequence analysis of E. festucae strains Fl1 and E2368; no accession stated for abcI

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: strain Fl1; strain E2368; related N. lolii sequences Lp19
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: E. festucae Fl1 infecting perennial ryegrass 'Nui'
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Non-ribosomal peptide synthetase (NRPS); Type II ferrichrome synthetase architecture with A-T-C modules and terminal T-C repeat
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Extracellular siderophore biosynthesis / ferrichrome-family siderophore pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Assembles the cyclic octapeptide siderophore epichloënin A by activating and incorporating three trans-AMHO residues plus one glutamine and four glycines
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Extracellular chelation and acquisition of iron required for fungal growth and maintenance of mutualistic symbiosis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes biosynthesis of the extracellular siderophore epichloënin A; loss abolishes epichloënin A/ferriepichloënin A production, disrupts iron homeostasis, and perturbs mutualistic interaction with perennial ryegrass
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for extracellular siderophore production; essential biosynthetic enzyme for epichloënin A formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Targeted gene disruption; complementation with full-length genomic sidN; LC-MS metabolite profiling in culture and mycelium; growth rescue by WT culture filtrate or iron; RT-PCR expression; in planta infection phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed under iron-depleted conditions; strongly repressed by iron supplementation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detectable in fungal cultures under iron depletion; not detected under iron-replete conditions
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Iron depletion; BPS chelation; FeSO4 or FeCl3 supplementation; H2O2 stress
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Extracellular culture supernatant/apoplast-associated extracellular fluid; ferri-form also detected in mycelium
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion causes severe growth defect under iron depletion, oxidative stress sensitivity, reduced sporulation, abnormal hyphal polarity/branching, plant stunting, altered hyphal localization, increased fungal biomass, and elevated ergovaline in planta
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to manage endophyte mutualism by tuning fungal iron acquisition; disruption would be detrimental to beneficial symbiosis and thus unsuitable where stable mutualism is desired
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequence comparison of strains Fl1 and E2368; custom Affymetrix GeneChip transcriptomics mentioned for ergovaline biosynthesis in WT vs ΔsidN plants (accession NA)
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. sidN encodes an NRPS required for biosynthesis of the novel extracellular siderophore epichloënin A. 2. ΔsidN mutants completely lack epichloënin A/ferriepichloënin A and accumulate the precursor trans-AMHO. 3. sidN is essential for growth under iron depletion and for maintaining controlled mutualistic growth of E. festucae in Lolium perenne. 4. sidN expression is repressed by iron. 5. Loss of sidN causes abnormal hyphal morphology, oxidative stress sensitivity, reduced sporulation, host stunting, and increased ergovaline accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Epichloënin A is a novel ferrichrome-family extracellular siderophore; ferriepichloënin A predominates in mycelial extracts, suggesting shuttle-based iron uptake. A3 domain experimentally shown previously to activate AMHO; this paper provides the genetic and metabolite evidence for the full pathway-product link.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: CCAAT-binding complex-associated iron homeostasis regulator HapX
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Iron availability/homeostasis linked to epichloënin A-mediated uptake
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Iron homeostasis; repression of iron-utilizing proteins during iron deficiency
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulated in ΔsidN-infected plants, indicating iron starvation response after loss of epichloënin A production
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller of iron deficiency response rather than direct determinant of epichloënin A biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Iron-utilizing proteins (general); exact direct targets not specified in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression of iron-utilizing proteins under iron deficiency
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Low intracellular iron; loss of extracellular siderophore-mediated iron uptake
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indicates activation of iron starvation program
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of iron homeostasis feedback under deficiency
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR in planta comparing WT and ΔsidN infections
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in ΔsidN-infected perennial ryegrass pseudostems relative to WT
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher transcript abundance in plants infected with ΔsidN mutants
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In planta symbiosis; inferred iron deficiency caused by sidN deletion
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Marker of fungal iron starvation status in symbiosis
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used here as an iron-status reporter, not directly manipulated.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. hapX was identified as an iron homeostasis regulator in E. festucae. 2. Its expression was significantly upregulated in plants infected with ΔsidN mutants. 3. This supports the conclusion that loss of epichloënin A disrupts fungal iron homeostasis.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Small GTPase regulator of NADPH oxidase
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Reactive oxygen species-related regulation connected indirectly to iron homeostasis phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Downregulated 2.2–3.3 fold in ΔsidN-infected plants, suggesting altered Nox regulatory state associated with abnormal hyphal growth
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Not a direct determinant of epichloënin A, but relevant regulator linked to the mutant symbiotic phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: NADPH oxidase complex / ROS spatial regulation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Altered iron homeostasis in ΔsidN background
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR in planta comparing WT and ΔsidN infections
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Reduced expression in ΔsidN mutant infections
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Lower transcript abundance in infected pseudostems with ΔsidN mutants
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the paper tests it experimentally in Results as a mechanistic regulator linked to the sidN phenotype, but it is not the central nutrient-metabolism gene of the study.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. racA expression was significantly downregulated in ΔsidN-infected plants. 2. This links loss of extracellular siderophore production with altered ROS regulatory circuitry. 3. No consistent colony ROS difference was detected despite altered racA transcript levels.
